user_message: Just came out of account health with Litware Inc. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-01-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is account health.

[00:05:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:13:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:16:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:21:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:26:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:30:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-O trap (Solution-value-design module structure assumes one-shot architecture rather than); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-O failure mode (Solution-value-design module structure assumes one-shot architecture rather than)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-O -- Solution-value-design module structure assumes one-shot architecture rather than
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
